reviews.review_text: Very fun co-op game, Constantly engaging and well designed enemies and boss fights. Some areas could have done with some more clear instructions and the ending felt....rushed. Overall, if you can pick it up on sale its a very worthwhile 15ish hours.

I love this game! I bought this to play with my friends that play switch. i did not know that it did not have crossplay to switch, so it sat unplayed in my library for 1 year. I saw it and i said "u know what, ill try it". I now love this game and play it everyday

I've always had a fondness for remnant from the first trailer I seen of it. Got it on release on Playstation and got it on steam once I moved to PC gaming. I managed to 100% it on Playstation (at the time before DLC achievements got introduced) and now I've managed to 100% on PC. I've introduced many friends to the game as well along the way and I never get bored of playing it over and over. Really is one of my favourite games!

This game is dope. I enjoyed the story. I enjoyed farming the weapons and trinkets. I enjoyed making builds to try and 1 shot bosses. Its a good. Better than Outriders. I recommend plaything this before the 2nd one they made. Extra fun if you can get a friend to grind with you once you hit adventure mode and beat the main story once.
Great atmosphere, bit dark at base lol. You can see how they improved and tigthened things up for the second game which is well worth playing.
great game, no issues even with low end potato level stuff. The only drawback(nothing major) is that the maps feel repetetive, but enjoyable regardless.
Fun game, story makes no sense but the bosses are fun.

Beat this game with a pal and we both upgraded our computers to play the second game. It turns out that was a horrible mistake lmao.

Still this game was very fun and I recommend it if you like playing roguelike fps games with friends.

i love this game, even though it was painful bc i genuinely suck, it was really enjoyable, id suggest this to anyone who likes souls games or wants to get into them, bosses are good, weapons are really fun to use and grind for, and the secret bosses, dont even get me started, overall 9/10
Would describe this game as a very fun and engaging RPG experience alongwith crazy third-person shooting.

Had a lot of fun playing this game, until it crashed and claimed that my profile was corrupted, leaving me no choice but to erase all of my progress. First time this has happened to me with any game. Pretty serious issue for a game that is all about grinding and upgrading equipment. Very disappointed as I was close to beating it for the first time. Doubt I'll ever go back, at this point.

SOULS LIKE WITH GUNS 

Pros: 
-Adventure mode to hasten collection of items such as rings and weapons. 
-Enemy Faction variety is diverse and interesting going from Plant demon monsters to straight up aliens. 
-Weapon Variety has a solid amount of strange and unique weapons to  use plus mods to add complexity.
-Melee+Guns is such an interesting concept that sets it apart from Souls games 
-Added content post release with  two DLC's 
-Build Variety via traits allow for various play styles even straight melee combat like in a typical souls game

Cons: 
-Short game despite added dlc and replay ability it's a lot of similar looking levels multiple times
-Buggy game especially on relase although I believe it's gotten much better even for linux users. 
-Samey looking UE4 engine.
-Story is somewhat interesting although cannot hold a candle to souls games.

It's honestly a really really good game. Ended up playing it with 2 other friends on a harder difficulty, and it very much forces you to collaborate with your teamates and use call outs. Its refreshingly difficult and collecting items to craft better gear feels rewarding. Would definitely recommend to anyone who is looking for an RPG shooter Co-op game.
i seriously love this game but God forbid i kill any more than 2 damn bosses in survival without the game crashing.
A bit of souls like DNA and touch of diablo loot/trait systems and rerunning of the bosses.

While the aesthetics of the game are quite captivating, Remnant suffers from lengthy maps with repetative enemy types. By far it's most agregious offense is it's bosses. Even playing with multiplayer, the bosses felt incredibly punishing to fight against but left no room to strategize or counter any of their attacks. Ultimately, every boss fight can be summarized by tanking their attacks (beacuse for whatever reason nearly every boss has unavoidable tracking projectiles) and hoping you have enough DPS to kill the eldrich horror first. Even the final boss was anticlimactic and lackluster. 

While it does not impact gameplay in the slightest, I feel I should also mention that the NPC and player dialog is some of the most cliche and mindnumbing I've ever heard. It felt comedic at first but quickly became stale and irritating. This statement could honestly summarize the game as a whole.

Os bosses são uns arrombados, tem bicho pra krl, vc tem que ter sorte de aparecer os itens que você quer
Mas é muito bom, recomendo

Hella fun
Lots to do
Replayability! 

Not worth full price IMO, get on sale!
Absolutely loved it! Probably gonna play more souls-like or monster hunter games because of Remnant.
it's fun with friends, only worth it if it's on sale imo not to mention remnant 2 exists

"Remnant: From the Ashes" is a surprisingly good action RPG with intense combat and an immersive atmosphere. The co-op works really well, and the variety of levels makes every playthrough feel unique.
First boss, very beginning of the game, is a skill check. I don't enjoy that kind of game.

A fantastic co-op experience that rewards good execution and punishes sloppy resource management. I ran this with two friends, and the gun-play loop is incredibly satisfying. You have enough build variety to match whatever operational role you want to fill in your squad. It’s challenging, but scaling that difficulty curve together is deeply rewarding. A necessary prerequisite before booting up the sequel.

To those that bought the game because it had the "soulslike" tag and were disappoined that it isnt exactly like Elden Ring: Sorry, that term doesn't mean anything. Never did. Play more games so you can make more accurate comparisons.
That being said, this game is pretty dull.
It can be difficult because the controls are clunky, the player character moves pretty sluggishly, the camera is bad, you often fight in cramped spaces, the game likes to spawn enemies behind or on top of you, and you don't really have any tools to deal with it at the start, on top of not understanding how things work.
Writing in general is awful. Do not expect coherent story or dialogue. The only characters i can remember are the ones i hate (though I'm not supposed to hate them, i think), not that they really matter.
There is no level design, only repetitive rooms, randomly chained together by procedural generation. And good level design is absolutely crucial for this kind of game, varied enemy placement is more important than plain enemy variety. Not that enemies are particularly varied.
Combat is bland. You can do dodge rolls, shoot and do melee attacks (normal and charged). Overall, combat is a bit more shallow than that of Skyrim.
No, I did not beat the game in 7 hours.
Interesting story line with amazing gameplay.

Lowkey garbage. Feels incomplete, the enemies are repetitive, and the cinematic scenes dont always work ok. It feels like an unreal engine demo more than a game. Or like the bare essentials to have the most basic of gameplay loops, repeated unto itself without deeper sustenance to provide; like a kart race track that is a perfect circle spiraling up across many levels of a building. Driving's fun, but all you'll ever do is turn the wheel once and hold it there.
dark souls and arc raiders made a baby that was cursed by wooden cthulu and blessed with world hopping, so far so good.

Gra trudna i nudna. Nie wiem jak online, ale singleplayer jest kiepski. Znudziłem się i nie chciało mi się kończyć gry, więc ocena negatywna. Ale tak po prawdzie gra nie jest jakaś tragicznie zła, po prostu nie moje klimaty. Jak dla mnie jest za trudna i za nudna. Owszem jest kilka ciekawych pomysłów, ale nie na tyle dobrych żeby przykuć moją uwagę na dłużej. W promocji można kupić i sobie spróbować, za cenę oryginalną nie polecam.
